message:
refactor(lib): remove dead `ensure_cass_origin` helper [y0dto]

Bead coding_agent_session_search-y0dto (mock-finder finding): the
helper at src/lib.rs:11641-11642 had `#[allow(dead_code)]` and zero
call sites. Confirmed via `rg -n 'ensure_cass_origin' src/ tests/
benches/` returning only the definition itself. The function was
scaffolding from an earlier provenance-enrichment slice that was
rolled back; provenance stamping now flows through
`normalized_search_hit_source_id` / `normalized_search_hit_origin_kind`
on the search-result side and through the rebuild state's own
origin fields on the indexing side.

Removed the function and its 38-line body. No new `unused import`
warnings: `crate::sources::provenance::SourceKind` was only used in
the removed signature and is imported locally inside each test
module that still references it (lib.rs:21340, 24530 both use
`use crate::sources::provenance::SourceKind` within a `mod tests`
block, unaffected by this removal).

Verification:
- `cargo check --all-targets` clean (via
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane3).
- `cargo test --lib` shows the dead-code removal is benign — the
  same 4 pre-existing rebuild_tantivy_from_db_* failures that were
  present before this change still occur (they trace back to the
  prep-profile step sequence change from commit 9e6c0ef7 / 9ct8r,
  filed separately).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
